title: oddkit_audit configured scope is writings/ only — canon/ is not in CI audit scope
content: Running oddkit_audit on the new canon files returned status=OK with zero findings, but the audit's configured scope is writings/ with excluded_prefixes=[docs/archive/]. The 39 files scanned were all under writings/. The new canon docs at canon/decisions/ and canon/principles/ were not actually audited. Manual URI integrity check on the seven URIs cited in the new docs (all ams:// references) confirmed each maps to an existing file. The audit tool's scope configuration is a CI choice that may warrant revisiting if canon/ should also be subject to dead-reference auditing.

title: oddkit_challenge surfaced legitimate gaps even on docs that pre-addressed many prerequisites
content: Running oddkit_challenge against both new canon docs at mode=peer-review-ready returned CHALLENGED status with block_until_addressed=false. The challenge tool fired on the summary input rather than the full document, so several of its missing-prerequisite flags (reversibility addressed; risks acknowledged; alternatives discussed) are already covered in the doc body. The legitimate gaps the challenge surfaced — counter-examples enumeration, retraction conditions, prior-art check, missing per-query-dynamic-orchestration cross-reference — were real and worth addressing before PR. The challenge tool's value is not in answering questions about the doc but in surfacing question categories the doc may not have engaged.

title: per-query-dynamic-orchestration is a sibling principle the original draft missed
content: The challenge surfaced ams://canon/principles/per-query-dynamic-orchestration as relevant canon. That principle argues why the wire is shaped to enable cheap dynamic composition (per-conversation, per-query latency budget). The new participation-replaces-integration principle argues why cheap participation collapses the connector category (across the ecosystem, over years). Same substrate framing, different layers of argument; complementary, not competing. The original draft did not cross-reference per-query-dynamic-orchestration in either complements or See Also; iteration adds both.

title: The historical pattern has counter-examples worth naming explicitly
content: Three open-substrate wins were cited in the original draft (SMTP, HTTP, chat federation). The challenge prompted enumeration of cases where closed substrates persisted: Facebook outlasting OpenSocial and diaspora* by ~15 years; iOS App Store consolidating against open mobile distribution; enterprise SSO consolidating around closed IdPs (Okta, Azure AD, Auth0) despite SAML/OIDC being open at the protocol layer; even SMTP itself being practically gatekept by Gmail/Outlook/Apple. The structural distinction that licenses the agent-comms bet — software-on-both-ends, no end-user network effects, third-party trust-and-safety per D0020, utility-billing economic model — is hypothesis-not-certainty.

title: Validation by tooled handoff is the structural separation; iteration scope is set by findings
content: The operating contract names oddkit_audit and oddkit_challenge as the tooled-handoff form of structural separation that makes self-review credible. Running them produced findings the same agent in the same session would not have surfaced unprompted (the per-query-dynamic-orchestration cross-ref miss; the audit-scope mismatch; the missing counter-examples). Iteration is then a fresh execution pass scoped by findings — not a re-debate of intent, not a re-derivation of the thesis. The findings define what changes; everything else stays. This is the rhythm: execution → validation (tooled or fresh-context) → iteration (scoped by findings) → accept.

title: Retraction conditions are the falsifiability test that separates principle from preference
content: Both new canon docs lacked named falsification conditions in the original draft. The challenge tool's framing — "a principle that cannot be falsified is a preference, not a principle" — applies equally to strategic decisions. Adding concrete retraction triggers (specific timescales, specific signal thresholds, specific countervailing evidence) converts hope-based commitments into evidence-bound commitments. The triggers are the mechanism by which canon stays honest as evidence accumulates rather than calcifying around its original framing.

title: Future canon principles must include retraction conditions in the body, not just the frontmatter stability tag
content: Frontmatter stability (semi_stable, stable) signals confidence but does not name what would falsify the claim. A retraction-conditions section in the doc body is the operational form of the falsifiability test and is required for any Tier-2 or Tier-1 principle that makes a substantive prediction. Tier-3 (deprecated/redirect) principles do not need retraction conditions because they are no longer load-bearing.

title: When oddkit_audit's scope misses the file under review, manual URI integrity check is the substitute
content: The audit tool's scope is CI-configured and may not cover newly added files in non-default directories. Trusting a status=OK from oddkit_audit without verifying the scope is a false-clean trap. The substitute is mechanical: extract every URI from the new file, resolve each to its expected file path, confirm existence. Cheap, deterministic, no false positives. Doing this manually does not replace the tool — it covers the gap the tool's configured scope leaves.

title: Iteration produced edits to both canon docs; ready for re-validation
content: canon/principles/participation-replaces-integration.md gained three new sections (Counter-Examples and What Makes Agent-Comms Structurally Different; Retraction Conditions; Prior Art and Distinction), an updated Outline reflecting them, and a per-query-dynamic-orchestration cross-reference in both complements (frontmatter) and See Also. canon/decisions/D0022-multi-brand-portfolio-on-shared-substrate.md gained concrete retraction triggers in the renamed "Reversibility and Retraction Triggers" section (brand-level retraction at 6 months without distinct audience signal; strategy-level retraction at 12 months across 3+ brands without statistical differentiation; substrate-attribution failure trigger; cross-brand confusion trigger). Both files re-staged to /mnt/user-data/outputs/. Awaiting operator re-review and PR direction.

title: Whether canon/ should be added to oddkit_audit CI scope is open
content: The audit's writings/-only scope is a deliberate CI choice (likely to keep the long-form essays' URI graph clean as the primary auditing concern). Whether canon/ files should also be in scope is a separate decision: pro = catches dead refs in canon edits earlier; con = canon/ is smaller and changes are PR-reviewed by default, possibly making CI audit redundant. Open question; not blocking.

title: canon/principles/participation-replaces-integration.md updated with three new sections and one cross-reference
content: Edits applied via str_replace: (1) frontmatter complements line extended to include ams://canon/principles/per-query-dynamic-orchestration; (2) Outline updated to list Counter-Examples, Retraction Conditions, and Prior Art and Distinction sections; (3) "Counter-Examples and What Makes Agent-Comms Structurally Different" section inserted after "The Historical Pattern: Email, Web, Chat" — enumerates Facebook/social, iOS/mobile, enterprise SSO, SMTP-post-substrate as cases where the historical pattern did not hold; (4) "Retraction Conditions" section inserted after "The Slow-Win Acknowledgment" — names four specific futures that would falsify the principle; (5) "Prior Art and Distinction" section inserted before "Implications for AMS Positioning" — cites end-to-end principle (Saltzer/Reed/Clark 1981) as architectural ancestor, names "Open standards win" / Metcalfe's Law / Aggregation Theory / "Protocols, Not Platforms" as adjacent prior art, distinguishes the integration-topology specificity of this principle from the broader open-vs-closed framing; (6) See Also extended with per-query-dynamic-orchestration cross-reference describing the layer relationship.

title: canon/decisions/D0022-multi-brand-portfolio-on-shared-substrate.md updated with concrete retraction triggers
content: Edits applied via str_replace: (1) Outline updated to reflect renamed section; (2) "Reversibility" section renamed to "Reversibility and Retraction Triggers"; (3) four named retraction triggers added with specific signal definitions (brand-level retraction after 6 months without distinct audience signal; strategy-level consolidation after 12 months across 3+ brands without statistical differentiation; substrate-attribution failure when visitors do not follow through to AMS; cross-brand confusion when audiences routinely conflate two brands). The retraction triggers turn the strategy from hope-based to evidence-bound and make falsification recognizable when warranted rather than postponed.

title: journal/2026-05-04-ams-multi-brand-validation-and-iteration.tsv committed
content: This journal entry. OLDC+H+E records of the validation cycle (audit + challenge findings) and the iteration that closed the gaps. Format matches existing journal TSV convention.
